L1.area: Data serialization and cross-task communication value handling layer

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where crafted cross-task communication payloads are fully deserialized without validation when retrieved by the web interface. This allows untrusted serialized data to trigger arbitrary class instantiation, potentially leading to remote code execution on the webserver.

L3.area: Operator extra links serialization and XCom value retrieval for the web API
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where crafted XCom payloads pushed by DAG authors are fully deserialized when the extra links API retrieves operator link values for display. Instead of safely converting the stored values to strings, the endpoint calls a deserialization method that can instantiate arbitrary classes from untrusted data, potentially leading to remote code execution on the webserver.

ground_truth.reason: The extra links API endpoint deserializes XCom values using XComModel.deserialize_value(), which can instantiate arbitrary classes from untrusted data crafted by DAG authors, enabling remote code execution on the webserver.
